clusters.title: Eight dead after US B-52 bomber crashes at California air force base
clusters.neutral_headline: US Air Force B-52 crashes in California
bullet_summary: m,” Edwards air force base said in a statement Monday afternoon

items.title: Eight dead after US B-52 bomber crashes at California air force base
items.standfirst: Eight people were killed after a US B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after take-off from Edwards Air Force Base in California on Monday. The aircraft was carrying a mix of military personnel, government employees and civilian contractors on a routine test mission when it went down, prompting an immediate investigation.
items.content: Eight people were killed after a US B-52 bomber crashed and burst into flames shortly after take-off from Edwards Air Force Base in California on Monday. The aircraft was carrying a mix of military personnel, government employees and civilian contractors on a routine test mission when it went down, prompting an immediate investigation.

items.content: Eight people are believed to have died after a US Air Force B-52 Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff

items.title: Eight presumed dead after B-52 bomber crashes at California air force base
items.standfirst: B-52 crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards air force base in southern California’s Mojave Desert, officials saySign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailEight people are presumed dead after a B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff on Monday morning at a US air force base in California’s Mojave Desert, officials said.“An Air Force B-52 Stratofortress carrying eight people on a routine test mission crashed today shortly after take-off at 11:20 a.m,” Edwards air force base said in a statement Monday afternoon. “Initial indications are that the crash was not survivable. “Emergency response personnel are on scene, and officials are working to account for all personnel.” Continue reading...
items.content: B-52 crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards air force base in southern California’s Mojave Desert, officials saySign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailEight people are presumed dead after a B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff on Monday morning at a US air force base in California’s Mojave Desert, officials said.“An Air Force B-52 Stratofortress carrying eight people on a routine test mission crashed today shortly after take-off at 11:20 a.m,” Edwards air force base said in a statement Monday afternoon. “Initial indications are that the crash was not survivable. “Emergency response personnel are on scene, and officials are working to account for all personnel.” Continue reading...

items.title: US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base

items.content: It was not immediately clear if there were any casualties in crash at base north of Los Angeles, California.

clusters.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for kids under 16
clusters.neutral_headline: Can a social media ban protect young users
bullet_summary: Several of the papers are leading on Sir Keir's plan to ban social media for under-16s in the UK, which was announced on Monday
Sites including Instagram, YouTube and TikTok will become inaccessible for millions of children, the prime minister has announced
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has announced a sweeping ban on social media use for those under 16, joining other countries around the world seeking to protect children online

items.title: U.K. announces plan to ban social media for kids under 16
items.standfirst: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a proposed law Monday that would ban anyone under 16 from social media apps like TikTok and Snapchat, and the social media companies themselves would be required to verify ages or face big fines. Leigh Kiniry has details from London.
items.content: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a proposed law Monday that would ban anyone under 16 from social media apps like TikTok and Snapchat, and the social media companies themselves would be required to verify ages or face big fines. Leigh Kiniry has details from London.

clusters.title: Oil prices hit three-month low and markets reach record high amid Iran deal breakthrough
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil prices hit three-month low and markets reach record...
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: Oil prices hit three-month low and markets reach record high amid Iran deal breakthrough
items.standfirst: Donald Trump posts ‘Let the oil flow’ as US-Iran peace deal sparks immediate drop for Brent crudeAnalysis: Oil and gas unlikely to return to prewar prices for months even if Hormuz reopensMiddle East crisis: live updatesGlobal oil prices have tumbled to a three-month low and stock markets closed at a record high amid fresh hopes that a US-Iran peace deal could end the greatest energy supply crisis in the history of the market.The price of Brent crude dropped about 4% to about $83 (£62) on Monday amid optimism that the strait of Hormuz could reopen shortly and bring a return of Gulf oil exports to the market. Wholesale gas prices fell 6% in Europe. Continue reading...
items.content: Donald Trump posts ‘Let the oil flow’ as US-Iran peace deal sparks immediate drop for Brent crudeAnalysis: Oil and gas unlikely to return to prewar prices for months even if Hormuz reopensMiddle East crisis: live updatesGlobal oil prices have tumbled to a three-month low and stock markets closed at a record high amid fresh hopes that a US-Iran peace deal could end the greatest energy supply crisis in the history of the market.The price of Brent crude dropped about 4% to about $83 (£62) on Monday amid optimism that the strait of Hormuz could reopen shortly and bring a return of Gulf oil exports to the market. Wholesale gas prices fell 6% in Europe. Continue reading...

items.title: Oil and gas unlikely to return to prewar prices for months even if Hormuz reopens
items.standfirst: Markets welcome US-Iran peace deal but prices may stay high as buyers race to refill depleted emergency crude stockpilesOil prices hit three-month low and markets rallyAfter more than 100 days of the greatest recorded disruption to the world’s energy supplies, the global oil and gas markets have breathed a sigh of relief.Hours after Donald Trump confirmed that a US-Iran peace deal would lead to the reopening of the strait of Hormuz for tankers carrying millions of barrels of oil and gas, the price of Brent crude tumbled to lows of $82 a barrel. Wholesale gas prices fell about 6%. Continue reading...
items.content: Markets welcome US-Iran peace deal but prices may stay high as buyers race to refill depleted emergency crude stockpilesOil prices hit three-month low and markets rallyAfter more than 100 days of the greatest recorded disruption to the world’s energy supplies, the global oil and gas markets have breathed a sigh of relief.Hours after Donald Trump confirmed that a US-Iran peace deal would lead to the reopening of the strait of Hormuz for tankers carrying millions of barrels of oil and gas, the price of Brent crude tumbled to lows of $82 a barrel. Wholesale gas prices fell about 6%. Continue reading...

bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Reuters Top News

items.title: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
items.standfirst: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.
items.content: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

items.title: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at ten million
items.standfirst: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]
items.content: Swiss voters have rejected a right-wing initiative that sought to limit the population to 10 million, with final results showing a 55% majority against it. The vote also came alongside approval of a civilian service reform, while debate over immigration and Switzerland’s ties with the EU remained central to the campaign. [swissinfo.ch]
